key_facts:
Resources covered by Azure Policy

 Although a policy can be assigned at the management group level, only resources at the subscription or resource group level are evaluated.
Only assign the minimum set of permissions necessary to perform a task and only grant these permissions to users who need permission.

Important

 To modify AVNM dynamic groups, you must be granted access via Azure RBAC role assignment only.
If your account was assigned only the Co-Administrator subscription role, you wouldn't have permissions on AVNM dynamic groups.

Some useful governance actions you can enforce with Azure Policy include:

 
 Ensure your team deploys Azure resources only to allowed regions.
After your business rules are formed, the policy definition or initiative is assigned to any scope of resources that Azure supports.

Remediate non-compliant resources

 While these effects primarily affect a resource when the resource is created or updated, Azure Policy also supports dealing with existing non-compliant resources without needing to alter that resource.
